summary: Validated post-fix state after manual MongoDB update (NYCOLLE valor 350 -> 85). Backend + frontend both PASS 100%. (1) POST /api/admin/auth/login (donas/Seinao10@@) -> 200 with token. (2) GET /api/admin/dashboard/kpis returns valor_total=85.0, valor_copiados=85.0, pix_gerados=1, pix_copiados=1, inscricoes=6 (no 350 anywhere). (3) GET /api/admin/inscriptions?limit=20 returns total=6, every item has valor=85.0 and taxa='R$ 85,00' — verified for all 6: ANA TESTE PASSAPORTE (333.333.333-03), JOAO TESTE CNH (222.222.222-02), MARIA TESTE RG (111.111.111-01), TESTE DOC E2E (555.444.333-22), CANDIDATO TESTE INTEGRACAO COMPLETA (111.222.333-44), NYCOLLE VICTÓRIA SANTIAGO DA SILVA (155.467.967-23). NYCOLLE specifically: valor=85.0, taxa='R$ 85,00', status='PIX copiado'. Zero documents with valor=350 or taxa='R$ 350,00'. (4) Frontend /donaspainel/#/inscricoes screenshot confirms: KPI cards 'VALOR TOTAL GERADO' R$ 85,00 (1 PIX gerado(s)) and 'PIX COPIADOS' R$ 85,00 (1 pix copiados), and NYCOLLE row VALOR column shows 'R$ 85,00 / PIX copiado'. body.innerText has ZERO occurrences of '350'. No code changes were made.
critical_code_review_comments: Root cause prevention note (informational, not blocking): the bug pattern (stale inscriptions persisting after a price change in /inscricao-passo8.html) can repeat in the future. Consider either (a) treating the 85.00 amount as a server-side constant / env var consumed by both the public page and the admin KPI aggregation, or (b) storing the price snapshot on the inscription at creation time and ALWAYS rendering it from there, so a future price change does not require a manual db.inscricoes.update_many. Currently KPIs sum db.inscricoes.valor field which is exactly what was migrated — works correctly today.

Minor non-blocking console warning observed on dashboard: 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' (Recharts ResponsiveContainer with no minHeight). Cosmetic, not related to this regression.
context_for_next_testing_agent: MongoDB fix for NYCOLLE (350->85) is fully effective. All 6 existing inscriptions now have valor=85.0. KPIs and admin painel render R$ 85,00 in all locations. No code change was needed/done. For future regressions of this kind, watch /api/admin/dashboard/kpis valor_total/valor_copiados and /api/admin/inscriptions items[*].valor. Frontend painel uses index.html app at /donaspainel/ with hash routes (#/login, #/inscricoes, #/cadastro, #/documentos). Login form has 2 plain <input> tags (no type='text'); use page.query_selector_all('input') and fill index 0 (user) + index 1 (password). KPI cards labels: 'VALOR TOTAL GERADO' and 'PIX COPIADOS'. NYCOLLE row text format: 'NYCOLLE VICTÓRIA SANTIAGO DA SILVA | LETRAS-PORTUGUÊS E FRANCÊS (LICENCIATURA) | 155.467.967-23 | Desktop | R$ 85,00 | PIX copiado | 23/06/2026, 22:19'.
rca of the issue: Original RCA (per main agent): NYCOLLE inscription was created BEFORE the public price was changed from R$ 350,00 to R$ 85,00 in /inscricao-passo8.html, so her db.inscricoes document had valor=350 frozen at creation. The admin KPI aggregator sums inscricoes.valor, so it kept showing R$ 350. Manual fix applied via db.inscricoes.update_many setting valor=85 on the stale record. Verified by GET /api/admin/dashboard/kpis (valor_total=85.0) and GET /api/admin/inscriptions (NYCOLLE.valor=85.0, taxa='R$ 85,00'). Mitigation suggestion in critical_code_review_comments above.
